events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need decide pass/fail. Scenario: improper fitting direction (using a vent tee on its back for drainage). IPC 704 likely addresses drainage fittings, tees, wyes, etc. Vent tee on its back for drainage is non-compliant. Work rough-complete. Decision fail. Risk? improper fitting direction can cause clogging, backups, maybe medium/high. Since drain slope & routing, vent tee used as drainage tee is code violation. Risk medium? Could cause drainage issues, solids accumulation. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 70. Confidence 1.0. Findings: vent tee installed horizontally/back for drainage, not allowed; improper fitting direction. Actions: remove/replace with proper drainage fitting (sanitary tee/wye/ combo) per IPC 704; ensure proper slope and orientation. Rationale: IPC 704 prohibits using vent tees in drainage position; vent tee on back allows solids to accumulate, impeding flow. References: IPC 704. Need include all fields. value 0. sound_source empty? string. event_time 0. rate 0. order empty list. workable empty list. Ensure valid JSON.

    "Improper fitting direction creates a ledge that can trap solids and obstruct drainage.",
    "Fitting does not comply with drainage fitting requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the incorrectly oriented vent tee.",
    "Replace with an approved drainage fitting (e.g., sanitary tee, wye, or combination fitting) oriented per IPC 704.",
    "Verify minimum slope and proper routing before close-out."
  ],
  "rationale": "IPC 704 requires drainage fittings to be arranged so that drainage flows freely without obstructions. A vent tee installed on its back for drainage creates an internal ledge, is not an approved drainage configuration, and is therefore non-compliant.",
  "references": ["IPC 704"]
}
